product.name: Linear
product.description: Project management software for software teams

competitors.name: Jira
competitors.pricing_details: Free forever for 10 users, with paid upgrades for larger teams
strengths: Recognized as a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant
Strong brand reputation with notable clients
Comprehensive feature set for project management
weaknesses: Limited information on custom pricing for large enterprises
May have a steep learning curve for new users
Dependence on Atlassian Community for support in free tier
competitors.market_position: Jira is a leading collaborative work management solution, recognized for its comprehensive feature set and strong brand reputation
competitors.target_audience: Jira targets a wide range of teams, including software development, marketing, IT, and project management teams of all sizes

competitors.name: Monday.com
competitors.pricing_details: Free plan available with unlimited time, paid plans start with 10 seats and offer yearly or monthly payment options
competitors.market_position: Monday.com is a leading work operating system that helps teams manage and automate their workflows, competing with other productivity and project management tools
competitors.target_audience: Monday.com targets teams and organizations of all sizes, from small businesses to large enterprises, across various industries

competitors.name: Asana
competitors.pricing_details: Free plan for personal projects, $10.99 per user per month billed annually for the Starter plan
strengths: Enterprise adoption with 85% of Fortune 100 companies
AI-powered features for efficient work management
weaknesses: Limited features in the free plan
Pricing may not be competitive for large teams
Dependence on integrations for full functionality
competitors.market_position: Asana is a leading work management platform that combines human and AI collaboration to help teams achieve their goals
competitors.target_audience: Asana targets teams and organizations of all sizes, from personal projects to large enterprises, across various industries including marketing, operations, IT, and leadership

competitors.name: Shortcut
competitors.market_position: Shortcut appears to be a company with a strong focus on branding and user experience, but its market position is unclear due to limited publicly available information.
competitors.target_audience: Shortcut likely targets businesses and teams looking for a streamlined and joyful work experience, but the specific target audience is not explicitly stated.
